title: pavonia clarkii
intro: Pavonia clarkii, also known as the Mexican rose or Clark's pavonia, is a species of flowering plant in the mallow family, Malvaceae. Traditionally, this plant is used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are derived from its roots, leaves, and stems. The health benefits of Pavonia clarkii include treating fever, colds, and respiratory issues, as well as skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
